nodes.name: UnityUtil.cs
nodes.summary: Static Unity helpers for enumerating scenes and GameObject hierarchies and querying modifier-key input state.
nodes.languageNotes: Lives in the ModestTree.Util namespace, a Zenject support library.

nodes.name: UnityUtil
nodes.summary: Static helper exposing scene enumeration, hierarchy traversal, and keyboard modifier-state checks for Unity.

nodes.name: ValidationUtil.cs
nodes.summary: Minimal static helper exposing whether the editor is currently running Zenject validation.

nodes.name: ValidationUtil
nodes.summary: Static utility reporting whether Zenject validation mode is active.

nodes.name: InitializableManager.cs
nodes.summary: Vendored Zenject runtime component that orders registered IInitializable instances by priority and invokes their Initialize once during container startup.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of the bundled Zenject DI framework under Plugins; not Aspid's own MVVM code.

nodes.name: InitializableManager
nodes.summary: Collects IInitializable bindings with optional priorities and runs them in sorted order, asserting against duplicate registrations.

nodes.name: Initialize
nodes.summary: Sorts initializables by priority, checks for duplicates, and invokes each Initialize inside a profiling block exactly once.

nodes.name: DecoratableMonoKernel.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the IDecoratableMonoKernel contract, a default implementation delegating to the tick/init/dispose managers, and a base decorator that forwards to a wrapped kernel.

nodes.name: IDecoratableMonoKernel
nodes.summary: Interface exposing the kernel lifecycle hooks (initialize, update, fixed/late update, dispose, late dispose) that can be decorated.

nodes.name: DecoratableMonoKernel
nodes.summary: Default IDecoratableMonoKernel that routes lifecycle calls to the injected TickableManager, InitializableManager, and DisposableManager.

nodes.name: BaseMonoKernelDecorator
nodes.summary: Abstract decorator that forwards every IDecoratableMonoKernel lifecycle call to the wrapped DecoratedMonoKernel.

nodes.name: DefaultGameObjectKernel.cs
nodes.summary: Trivial concrete MonoKernel attached to GameObjects so their bound tickables/initializables run with the standard Unity lifecycle.

nodes.name: DefaultGameObjectKernel
nodes.summary: Concrete MonoKernel subclass with no additional behavior, used as the default kernel for GameObject contexts.

nodes.name: Kernel.cs
nodes.summary: Non-MonoBehaviour kernel that wires the tickable, initializable, and disposable managers behind the standard Zenject lifecycle interfaces.

nodes.name: Kernel
nodes.summary: Implements IInitializable/ITickable/ILateTickable/IFixedTickable/IDisposable/ILateDisposable by delegating each callback to the injected managers.

nodes.name: MonoKernel.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract MonoBehaviour kernel that drives the injected managers from Unity's Start/Update/FixedUpdate/LateUpdate/OnDestroy, optionally delegating to a decoratable kernel.

nodes.name: MonoKernel
nodes.summary: Base MonoBehaviour kernel bridging Unity message methods to TickableManager, InitializableManager, and DisposableManager, with optional decoratable-kernel override.

nodes.name: ProjectKernel.cs
nodes.summary: Project-context MonoKernel that tears down all scene contexts in reverse load order on application quit to guarantee deterministic disposal.

nodes.name: ProjectKernel
nodes.summary: MonoKernel for the project container that force-unloads scenes and destroys their contexts in deterministic order when the application quits.

nodes.name: SceneKernel.cs
nodes.summary: Scene-level MonoKernel that logs aggregated profiling results after the base Start completes.

nodes.name: SceneKernel
nodes.summary: Concrete MonoKernel for scene contexts that emits formatted profile timer results on Start.

nodes.name: PoolableManager.cs
nodes.summary: Vendored Zenject manager with six arity-specific generic variants that order IPoolable bindings by priority and fire OnSpawned/OnDespawned with profiling.
nodes.languageNotes: Six near-identical partial-arity overloads (0..5 spawn parameters) generated to support memory pools without boxing.

nodes.name: PoolableManager
nodes.summary: Family of generic PoolableManager classes (0 to 5 spawn args) that sort poolables by priority and dispatch OnSpawned/OnDespawned in profiled order.

nodes.name: SceneContextRegistry.cs
nodes.summary: Registry mapping Unity scenes to their SceneContext and DiContainer, with name- and scene-based lookups plus try-get variants.

nodes.name: SceneContextRegistry
nodes.summary: Keeps a scene-to-SceneContext map and resolves the SceneContext or DiContainer for a given scene or scene name.

nodes.name: SceneContextRegistryAdderAndRemover.cs
nodes.summary: Lifecycle helper that registers a SceneContext with the SceneContextRegistry on initialize and removes it on dispose.

nodes.name: SceneContextRegistryAdderAndRemover
nodes.summary: IInitializable/IDisposable that adds its SceneContext to the registry on init and removes it on dispose.

nodes.name: TaskUpdater.cs
nodes.summary: Generic priority-ordered task scheduler plus concrete updaters for ITickable, ILateTickable, and IFixedTickable that invoke each task in priority ranges.

nodes.name: TaskUpdater
nodes.summary: Abstract generic base that maintains a priority-sorted task list, queues additions, prunes removed tasks, and updates within priority ranges.

nodes.name: TickablesTaskUpdater
nodes.summary: Concrete TaskUpdater for ITickable that calls Tick on each scheduled task.

nodes.name: LateTickablesTaskUpdater
nodes.summary: Concrete TaskUpdater for ILateTickable that calls LateTick on each scheduled task.

nodes.name: FixedTickablesTaskUpdater
nodes.summary: Concrete TaskUpdater for IFixedTickable that calls FixedTick on each scheduled task.

nodes.name: TickableManager.cs
nodes.summary: Central manager that registers tickables/late-tickables/fixed-tickables with priorities and drives the corresponding TaskUpdaters each frame, honoring a pause flag.

nodes.name: TickableManager
nodes.summary: Builds priority-sorted tickable collections, owns the three TaskUpdaters, and dispatches Update/FixedUpdate/LateUpdate unless paused.

nodes.name: Update
nodes.summary: Lazily initializes the tickables list and drives the standard-update TaskUpdater when not paused.

nodes.name: ActionInstaller.cs
nodes.summary: Tiny Zenject Installer that runs a supplied Action<DiContainer> delegate as its InstallBindings body.

nodes.name: ActionInstaller
nodes.summary: Installer subclass that invokes an injected install delegate against the container.

nodes.name: ZenAutoInjecter.cs
nodes.summary: Zenject DI utility MonoBehaviour that automatically injects its GameObject by looking up the appropriate scene or parent container on Awake.
nodes.languageNotes: Vendored Zenject library code; resolves containers via SceneContextRegistry or parent Context components.

nodes.name: ZenAutoInjecter
nodes.summary: MonoBehaviour that auto-injects its GameObject from the resolved DI container, choosing the container source from the current scene or a parent Context.

nodes.name: ZenjectException.cs
nodes.summary: Defines the framework-specific exception type thrown by Zenject for dependency-injection errors.

nodes.name: ZenjectSceneLoader.cs
nodes.summary: Zenject scene loading helper that prepares DI bindings before loading scenes synchronously or asynchronously via Unity's SceneManager.
nodes.languageNotes: Tree-sitter did not capture the class wrapper; methods LoadScene/LoadSceneAsync/PrepareForLoadScene drive container-aware scene loading.

nodes.name: ZenjectStateMachineBehaviourAutoInjecter.cs
nodes.summary: MonoBehaviour that injects all StateMachineBehaviour instances attached to an Animator using the resolved Zenject container.

nodes.name: ZenjectStateMachineBehaviourAutoInjecter
nodes.summary: Receives the DI container via Construct and injects each StateMachineBehaviour on the Animator during Start.

nodes.name: ZenPools.cs
nodes.summary: Static object-pooling helper that spawns and despawns reusable Zenject internals (InjectContext, BindInfo, BindStatement, collections, arrays) to minimize allocations.
nodes.languageNotes: Vendored Zenject pooling layer wrapping generic StaticMemoryPool/ListPool/ArrayPool primitives.

nodes.name: ZenPools
nodes.summary: Static pool facade providing Spawn/Despawn methods for InjectContext, lists, hashsets, dictionaries, arrays, and Zenject bind metadata objects.

nodes.name: ZenReflectionTypeAnalyzer.cs
nodes.summary: Reflection-based type analyzer that discovers inject constructors, methods, fields, and properties for Zenject's runtime dependency resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Vendored Zenject reflection backend; scans members carrying registered inject-attribute types.

nodes.name: ReflectionTypeAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Builds ReflectionTypeInfo by reflecting over a type's inject-marked constructors, methods, fields, and properties, with WP8 generated-constructor filtering.

nodes.name: GetReflectionInfo
nodes.summary: Assembles a complete ReflectionTypeInfo for a type by gathering its inject constructor, methods, fields, and properties.

nodes.name: GetMethodInfos
nodes.summary: Collects inject-marked instance methods and converts their parameters into InjectableInfo entries.

nodes.name: TryGetInjectConstructor
nodes.summary: Selects the constructor to use for injection, preferring an inject-attributed constructor, otherwise the sole public or smallest-arity constructor, with Component/abstract guards.

nodes.name: CreateInjectableInfoForParam
nodes.summary: Builds an InjectableInfo descriptor for a constructor/method parameter from its inject attribute metadata.

nodes.name: GetInjectableInfoForMember
nodes.summary: Builds an InjectableInfo descriptor for an inject-marked field or property member.

nodes.name: ZenUtilInternal.cs
nodes.summary: Internal Zenject utility class with scene/GameObject traversal, injectable MonoBehaviour discovery, inheritance-delta computation, and null/delegate comparison helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Vendored Zenject internals; several methods are conditionally compiled and not captured as top-level by tree-sitter.

nodes.name: ZenUtilInternal
nodes.summary: Static helper for locating scene contexts, gathering injectable MonoBehaviours under GameObjects, adding StateMachineBehaviour auto-injecters, and computing type inheritance depth.

nodes.name: GetInheritanceDelta
nodes.summary: Computes the number of inheritance steps between a derived type and a parent type or interface.

nodes.name: IValidatable.cs
nodes.summary: Interface marking Zenject installers/objects as validatable so the container can verify bindings without resolving them.

nodes.name: ValidationMarker.cs
nodes.summary: Sentinel value substituted for real instances during Zenject validation runs, recording the marked type and whether instantiation failed.

nodes.name: zenject.asmdef
nodes.summary: Unity assembly definition that compiles the vendored Zenject plugin source into its own assembly.

nodes.name: AuthenticationBase.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base for Asset Store authentication strategies that builds the shared form content and parses the authentication HTTP response.

nodes.name: AuthenticationBase
nodes.summary: Abstract IAuthenticationType implementation supplying common auth form content (license/hardware hashes) and response parsing for concrete auth types.

nodes.name: IAssetStoreApi.cs
nodes.summary: Interface declaring the Asset Store API surface: authentication, package and category retrieval, thumbnails, metadata refresh, and uploads.

nodes.name: IAssetStoreClient.cs
nodes.summary: Interface for the low-level Asset Store HTTP client abstracting session handling and Get/Post/Put/Send requests.

nodes.name: IAuthenticationType.cs
nodes.summary: Interface defining a single Authenticate method for pluggable Asset Store authentication strategies.

nodes.name: IPackageUploader.cs
nodes.summary: Interface defining the package upload contract used by the Asset Store API to publish packages.

nodes.name: PackageUploaderBase.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base for package uploaders providing chunked-upload helpers, success validation, and polling for upload completion with timeout.

nodes.name: PackageUploaderBase
nodes.summary: Abstract IPackageUploader implementation with shared response validation and a polling loop that waits for the server to finish processing the upload.

nodes.name: WaitForUploadCompletion
nodes.summary: Polls the upload response stream, reporting progress and throwing a TimeoutException if the server does not respond within the configured window.

nodes.name: ApiUtility.cs
nodes.summary: Static helpers for the Asset Store API: building query URIs, combining main/extra/category package data, and producing license and hardware hashes.

nodes.name: ApiUtility
nodes.summary: Static utility class assembling Asset Store request URIs, merging package metadata sources, and deriving auth-token-based hashes.

nodes.name: CreateUri
nodes.summary: Builds a Uri from a base URL plus query parameters, optionally appending the default Asset Store query and URL-escaping values.

nodes.name: CombinePackageData
nodes.summary: Merges main package data with extra package data and category lookups into a single enriched package model.

nodes.name: AssetStoreApi.cs
nodes.summary: Concrete Asset Store API client orchestrating authentication, package/category retrieval, thumbnail and metadata fetching, and package uploads over the HTTP client.

nodes.name: AssetStoreApi
nodes.summary: IAssetStoreApi implementation that wraps IAssetStoreClient to authenticate, fetch packages/categories/thumbnails, refresh metadata, and upload packages, returning typed response objects.

nodes.name: GetPackages
nodes.summary: Fetches main and extra package data plus categories and combines them via ApiUtility into a single PackagesDataResponse.

nodes.name: RefreshPackageMetadata
nodes.summary: Refreshes a single package's metadata by re-fetching its extra data and category, returning a deep-cloned updated Package model.

nodes.name: Authenticate
nodes.summary: Runs the supplied authentication strategy and, on success, stores the returned session id on the HTTP client.

nodes.name: GetLatestAssetStoreToolsVersion
nodes.summary: Queries the Asset Store endpoint for the latest tools version and returns a typed version response.

nodes.name: GetPackageThumbnail
nodes.summary: Downloads a package's thumbnail image bytes from its icon URL and wraps them in a thumbnail response.

nodes.name: GetCategories
nodes.summary: Retrieves the list of Asset Store package categories and returns a typed category response.

nodes.name: GetPackageUploadedVersions
nodes.summary: Fetches the Unity versions a package has already been uploaded under, returning a typed version data response.

nodes.name: UploadPackage
nodes.summary: Delegates to an IPackageUploader to perform the upload and wraps the outcome in a PackageUploadResponse.

nodes.name: AssetStoreClient.cs
nodes.summary: Thin HttpClient wrapper implementing the Asset Store client contract with session-id header management and Get/Post/Put/Send helpers.

nodes.name: AssetStoreClient
nodes.summary: IAssetStoreClient implementation that configures an HttpClient, manages the session-id default header, and exposes Get/Post/Put/Send request methods.

nodes.name: CloudTokenAuthentication.cs
nodes.summary: Authentication strategy that logs into the Asset Store using a Unity cloud token via the shared authentication base.

nodes.name: CloudTokenAuthentication
nodes.summary: AuthenticationBase subclass that posts a cloud-token credential to the login endpoint and parses the response.

nodes.name: CredentialsAuthentication.cs
nodes.summary: Authentication strategy that logs into the Asset Store using email and password credentials via the shared authentication base.

nodes.name: CredentialsAuthentication
nodes.summary: AuthenticationBase subclass that posts email/password credentials to the login endpoint and parses the response.

nodes.name: Category.cs
nodes.summary: Data model for an Asset Store category with custom JSON property resolvers mapping snake_case API fields to C# properties.

nodes.name: Category
nodes.summary: POCO representing an Asset Store category (Id, Name, Status) with nested contract resolvers for JSON deserialization.

nodes.name: Package.cs
nodes.summary: Data model for an Asset Store package with identity, status, paths, size, and icon fields plus custom JSON property resolvers.

nodes.name: Package
nodes.summary: POCO describing an Asset Store package (ids, name, status, category, paths, size, icon URL) with nested resolvers mapping snake_case JSON to properties.

nodes.name: PackageAdditionalData.cs
nodes.summary: Supplementary Asset Store package data model (category id, modified time, size) merged into the primary Package model.

nodes.name: PackageAdditionalData
nodes.summary: POCO holding extra package fields (CategoryId, Modified, Size) with a nested JSON contract resolver.

nodes.name: User.cs
nodes.summary: Data model for an authenticated Asset Store user (id, session, publisher info) with a debug ToString and JSON property resolver.

nodes.name: User
nodes.summary: POCO representing an authenticated Asset Store user with identity and publisher fields, a diagnostic ToString override, and a JSON contract resolver.

nodes.name: AssetStoreResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Base Asset Store API response model that captures success/cancel/exception state and validates raw JSON for error and status fields.

nodes.name: AssetStoreResponse
nodes.summary: Base response type exposing Success/Cancelled/Exception state with a ValidateAssetStoreResponse helper that throws on API error/status payloads.

nodes.name: ValidateAssetStoreResponse
nodes.summary: Deserializes the JSON body and throws an Exception when the response contains an error or a non-OK status message.

nodes.name: AssetStoreToolsVersionResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model that parses the latest Asset Store Tools version string from the API payload for update checks.

nodes.name: AssetStoreToolsVersionResponse
nodes.summary: Parses the tools version from JSON, reusing base validation and exposing the resolved Version property.

nodes.name: AuthenticationResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Authentication response model that deserializes login user data and validates required login and publisher fields.

nodes.name: AuthenticationResponse
nodes.summary: Holds the deserialized User and validates login credentials and publisher presence, throwing on incomplete authentication data.

nodes.name: CategoryDataResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model that deserializes the Asset Store category list from a JSON array into typed Category objects.

nodes.name: CategoryDataResponse
nodes.summary: Parses a JSON array of categories into a populated Categories list using a Json.NET serializer.

nodes.name: PackagesAdditionalDataResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model parsing per-package extra metadata (latest version id, modified date, size) from the Asset Store packages payload.

nodes.name: PackagesAdditionalDataResponse
nodes.summary: Validates and parses the packages JSON, extracting each package's latest version metadata into a list of PackageAdditionalData.

nodes.name: ParseExtraData
nodes.summary: Reads the packages array from JSON and projects each entry's latest version id, modified timestamp, and size into PackageAdditionalData items.

nodes.name: PackagesDataResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model parsing the publisher's main package listing from the Asset Store packages JSON into typed Package objects.

nodes.name: PackagesDataResponse
nodes.summary: Validates and iterates the packages JSON object, deserializing each property value into a Package and collecting them into the Packages list.

nodes.name: ParseMainData
nodes.summary: Deserializes each package property of the JSON object into Package instances, skipping entries with empty identifiers.

nodes.name: PackageThumbnailResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model that loads downloaded thumbnail bytes into a Unity Texture2D for display in the uploader UI.

nodes.name: PackageUploadedUnityVersionDataResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model parsing the list of Unity versions a package was uploaded under from the API content payload.

nodes.name: PackageUploadedUnityVersionDataResponse
nodes.summary: Validates the response and extracts the unity_versions array from the content object into the UnityVersions list.

nodes.name: RefreshedPackageDataResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Lightweight response wrapper holding a single refreshed Package result returned by the Asset Store API.

nodes.name: UploadResponse.cs
nodes.summary: Response model for a package upload carrying the resulting UploadStatus after validating the API payload.

nodes.name: SessionAuthentication.cs
nodes.summary: Authentication strategy that authenticates against the Asset Store API using a previously issued session id.

nodes.name: SessionAuthentication
nodes.summary: Builds session-id authentication content and posts it to the API client, parsing the response asynchronously.

nodes.name: UnityPackageUploader.cs
nodes.summary: Uploads a built .unitypackage to the Asset Store via the API, streaming the file with progress reporting and polling for completion.

nodes.name: UnityPackageUploader
nodes.summary: Validates upload settings, PUTs the package stream to the upload URI, and returns a PackageUploadResponse reflecting success, cancellation, or failure.

nodes.name: UploadTask
nodes.summary: Streams the package file as StreamContent over HTTP PUT, waits for completion, and maps the API result or exceptions to a PackageUploadResponse.

nodes.name: UploadStatus.cs
nodes.summary: Enum defining the possible outcome states of a package upload operation.

nodes.name: AssemblyInfo.cs
nodes.summary: Assembly-level metadata declaration exposing internal types of the Asset Store Tools editor assembly to related assemblies.

nodes.name: AssetStoreTools.cs
nodes.summary: Editor menu entry point exposing commands to open the uploader, validator, preview generator, settings, and external portals.

nodes.name: AssetStoreTools
nodes.summary: Static facade with MenuItem-driven methods that resolve and show the various Asset Store Tools editor windows and open documentation URLs.

nodes.name: AssetStoreToolsWindow.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base EditorWindow for Asset Store Tools windows providing a default title and an Init lifecycle hook.

nodes.name: AssetStoreToolsWindow
nodes.summary: Base EditorWindow that sets a default window title on enable and defines an abstract Init for derived tool windows.

nodes.name: Constants.cs
nodes.summary: Central constants for the Asset Store Tools: API endpoints, query defaults, URLs, and a command-line argument resolver.

nodes.name: Constants
nodes.summary: Static container of nested constant groups (API URLs, default queries, project paths) plus a GetArgument helper for reading command-line overrides.

nodes.name: IPackageExporter.cs
nodes.summary: Interface defining the package exporter contract with an Export method and exporter Settings.

nodes.name: IPackageExporter
nodes.summary: Contract for package exporters exposing an Export operation and the active exporter Settings.

nodes.name: IPreviewInjector.cs
nodes.summary: Interface for injecting generated asset previews into an exported package via an Inject method.

nodes.name: PackageExporterBase.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base exporter implementing IPackageExporter with shared settings validation, asset path gathering, and GUID resolution from .meta files.

nodes.name: PackageExporterBase
nodes.summary: Abstract exporter providing the Export template, settings validation, recursive asset path collection, and GUID resolution (including plugin-folder GUID generation), delegating actual export to ExportImpl.

nodes.name: GetAssetGuid
nodes.summary: Resolves an asset's GUID via AssetDatabase, generating a new one for plugin folders or scraping it from the .meta file when needed.

nodes.name: GetAssetPaths
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a root directory to collect all file and subdirectory asset paths with normalized separators.

nodes.name: PackageExporterSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base settings type for package exporters defining the output filename.

nodes.name: DefaultExporterSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Settings for the default exporter specifying export paths, dependencies, and the preview generator to use.

nodes.name: DefaultPackageExporter.cs
nodes.summary: Default exporter that builds a temp package structure, generates and injects previews, and compresses it into a .unitypackage via an external 7-Zip process.

nodes.name: DefaultPackageExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete PackageExporterBase that assembles a temporary package tree with manifests, generates and injects asset previews, and archives the result via platform-specific 7-Zip invocations.

nodes.name: Export
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the export pipeline: saves assets, builds the temp structure, generates and injects previews, archives the package, and reveals the result.

nodes.name: CreateTempPackageStructure
nodes.summary: Lays out the Unity-package temp directory: copies each asset and its .meta by GUID, writes pathname files, and emits package manifest JSON.

nodes.name: CreateUnityPackageUniversal
nodes.summary: Tars and gzips the temp package directory by invoking the bundled 7-Zip executable in two passes, throwing on non-zero exit codes.

nodes.name: StartProcess
nodes.summary: Launches an external process with the given arguments and working directory, sets environment variables, and waits for it to exit.

nodes.name: LegacyExporterSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Settings for the legacy exporter specifying export paths and whether to include dependencies.

nodes.name: LegacyPackageExporter.cs
nodes.summary: Legacy exporter that delegates packaging to Unity's internal AssetExportPackage method via reflection and waits for the output file.

nodes.name: LegacyPackageExporter
nodes.summary: Concrete PackageExporterBase that resolves and invokes Unity's internal package export method by reflection, collecting GUIDs and polling for the produced file.

nodes.name: Export
nodes.summary: Collects asset GUIDs, reflectively invokes Unity's AssetExportPackage method, then polls until the output package file exists before returning the result.

nodes.name: GetGuids
nodes.summary: Resolves the GUIDs for the configured export paths, optionally restricting to folders, by reusing the base asset path and GUID helpers.

nodes.name: ISceneScreenshotter.cs
nodes.summary: Interface defining scene screenshotting capability with Settings and overloaded Screenshot methods.

nodes.name: ISceneScreenshotter
nodes.summary: Contract exposing Settings plus Screenshot overloads for capturing a scene or a positioned target to an output path.

nodes.name: MaterialScreenshotter.cs
nodes.summary: Scene screenshotter that frames the camera around a material preview target using its renderer bounds.

nodes.name: MaterialScreenshotter
nodes.summary: Concrete SceneScreenshotterBase that positions the camera around a material preview sphere by computing global bounds and orbiting the view.

nodes.name: PositionCamera
nodes.summary: Computes the target's global bounds and orbits the camera to frame the material preview for a screenshot.

nodes.name: MeshScreenshotter.cs
nodes.summary: Scene screenshotter that frames the camera around a mesh/model preview target using its combined renderer bounds.

nodes.name: MeshScreenshotter
nodes.summary: Concrete SceneScreenshotterBase that positions the camera around a mesh/model preview by computing global bounds and orbiting the view.

nodes.name: PositionCamera
nodes.summary: Computes the target's global bounds and orbits the camera to frame the mesh preview for a screenshot.

nodes.name: SceneScreenshotterBase.cs
nodes.summary: Abstract base implementing ISceneScreenshotter: validates settings, acquires a camera, positions lighting, captures the scene, and writes the resized texture to disk.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a template-method flow where subclasses only override PositionCamera while base handles capture, resize, conversion, and file output.

nodes.name: SceneScreenshotterBase
nodes.summary: Abstract ISceneScreenshotter implementation providing camera acquisition, settings validation, lighting setup, bounds normalization, and texture capture/write; subclasses supply PositionCamera.

nodes.name: Screenshot
nodes.summary: Validates settings, captures the camera into a texture, resizes and converts it to the requested format, and writes the bytes to the output path.

nodes.name: GetGlobalBounds
nodes.summary: Encapsulates all renderer bounds into a single combined Bounds for camera framing.

nodes.name: ValidateSettings
nodes.summary: Throws ArgumentException when screenshot settings (dimensions, depth, format) are invalid before capture.

nodes.name: SceneScreenshotterSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Settings data class supplying width/height/depth, native dimensions, and format to scene screenshotters.

nodes.name: SceneScreenshotterSettings
nodes.summary: Holds Width, Height, Depth, NativeWidth, NativeHeight, and Format consumed by SceneScreenshotterBase during capture.

nodes.name: AudioTypeGeneratorSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Settings data class supplying texture dimensions, waveform sample/background colors, and format to the audio preview generator.

nodes.name: AudioTypeGeneratorSettings
nodes.summary: Holds Width, Height, SampleColor, BackgroundColor, and Format used by AudioTypePreviewGenerator to render waveform previews.

nodes.name: AudioTypePreviewGenerator.cs
nodes.summary: Type preview generator that collects audio clips and renders a waveform texture per clip, writing it out as a preview image.
nodes.languageNotes: Reads raw PCM samples via AudioClip.GetData, builds AudioChannel waveforms, and paints them column-by-column onto a Texture2D.

nodes.name: AudioTypePreviewGenerator
nodes.summary: TypePreviewGeneratorBase subclass that collects supported AudioClips, decodes their samples into AudioChannels, and draws waveform previews onto a texture written to disk.

nodes.name: CollectAssets
nodes.summary: Finds AudioClip assets in the input paths, filtering to main assets with a supported load type.

nodes.name: GenerateImpl
nodes.summary: Iterates collected audio clips, generates a waveform texture for each, converts and writes it, and reports progress via OnAssetProcessed.

nodes.name: GenerateAudioClipTexture
nodes.summary: Loads audio data, (re)initializes the texture, fills background and waveform foreground, applies pixels, then unloads the clip.

nodes.name: CreateChannels
nodes.summary: Splits per-channel samples into vertically partitioned AudioChannel instances for stacked waveform drawing.

nodes.name: GetChannelSamples
nodes.summary: Reads interleaved PCM data from the AudioClip and de-interleaves it into per-channel sample lists.

nodes.name: ITypePreviewGenerator.cs
nodes.summary: Interface for type-specific preview generators exposing Settings and a Generate method.

nodes.name: ITypePreviewGenerator
nodes.summary: Contract exposing Settings and Generate() implemented by the concrete type preview generators.

nodes.name: MaterialTypePreviewGenerator.cs
nodes.summary: Scene-based type preview generator that applies each material to a primitive sphere and screenshots it, skipping materials with invalid shaders.

nodes.name: MaterialTypePreviewGenerator
nodes.summary: TypePreviewGeneratorFromScene subclass that collects valid materials, applies them to a sphere primitive, and captures a screenshot via the configured Screenshotter.

nodes.name: GeneratePreviewsInScene
nodes.summary: Creates a primitive sphere, assigns each material to it, screenshots the result, and records preview metadata while yielding between assets.

nodes.name: CollectAssets
nodes.summary: Finds Material assets in the input paths, keeping main assets whose shaders are valid.

nodes.name: ModelTypePreviewGenerator.cs
nodes.summary: Scene-based type preview generator that instantiates each model, swaps in a default shader, and screenshots it for a preview image.

nodes.name: ModelTypePreviewGenerator
nodes.summary: TypePreviewGeneratorFromScene subclass that collects renderable models, instantiates them with a default shader, screenshots each, and emits preview metadata.

nodes.name: GeneratePreviewsInScene
nodes.summary: Instantiates each model, replaces its shaders with the default, screenshots it via the Screenshotter, then destroys it and records metadata.

nodes.name: ReplaceShaders
nodes.summary: Swaps every renderer's materials on the instantiated model to use the provided default shader.

nodes.name: PrefabTypePreviewGenerator.cs
nodes.summary: Scene-based type preview generator that instantiates each prefab, fixes missing shaders, simulates particle systems, and screenshots the result.

nodes.name: PrefabTypePreviewGenerator
nodes.summary: TypePreviewGeneratorFromScene subclass that instantiates renderable prefabs, repairs missing object/particle shaders, simulates particle systems, screenshots, and records metadata.

nodes.name: GeneratePreviewsInScene
nodes.summary: Instantiates each prefab, replaces missing shaders, handles particle systems, screenshots it, then destroys the instance and records preview metadata.

nodes.name: ReplaceMissingShaders
nodes.summary: Detects renderers with missing/error shaders and reassigns them the appropriate default object or particle shader.

nodes.name: HandleParticleSystems
nodes.summary: Stops, clears, and simulates each particle system on the instantiated prefab so previews show a representative frame.

nodes.name: TextureTypeGeneratorSettings.cs
nodes.summary: Settings data class supplying maximum width/height and format for texture preview generation.

nodes.name: TextureTypeGeneratorSettings
nodes.summary: Holds MaxWidth, MaxHeight, and Format used by the texture preview generator to downscale source textures.

nodes.name: PackageExporterResult.cs
nodes.summary: Plain data class carrying the outcome of an Asset Store package export, including success flag, exported path, preview-generation result, and any thrown exception.

nodes.name: PackageExporterResult

nodes.name: AutomatedTest

nodes.name: ValidationTestScriptableObject
nodes.summary: ScriptableObject asset storing a validation test case's id, title, description, category info, validation type, and backing MonoScript reference.

nodes.summary: ITestScript classifying packaged archive files by signature and name into allowed, problematic, and disallowed categories.

nodes.summary: ITestScript flagging prefabs that have no components other than the root Transform.

nodes.name: CheckFileMenuNames
nodes.summary: ITestScript inspecting [MenuItem] attributes across scripts and assemblies, reporting menu paths that do not begin with an acceptable top-level menu.

nodes.summary: ITestScript reporting prefabs with missing mesh references and model meshes that are never referenced by any prefab.

edges.target: step:.github/workflows/release.yml:Verify committed generator DLLs match the freshly-built ones

layers.name: Core Framework Source
layers.description: The MVVM framework core under Source/: IViewModel, bindable members, binder base types, commands, bind modes, and source-generation attributes that define the framework contract.

layers.name: StarterKit
layers.description: Ready-to-use Unity UI binders (Text, Image, Toggle, Slider, etc.), value converters, observable collection binders, and DynamicViewModel built on top of the core framework.

layers.name: Unity Integration
layers.description: Unity runtime glue and Editor tooling under Unity/: View components, binder inspectors, UIToolkit USS styles, and debug panels that wire the framework into the Unity Editor.

layers.name: Samples
layers.description: Example projects (HelloWorld, Stats, VirtualizedList, Counter, Greeter, ExampleScripts) demonstrating framework usage for manual validation and onboarding.

layers.name: Vendored: Zenject
layers.description: Third-party Zenject dependency-injection framework bundled under Plugins/Zenject; integration target, not maintained as part of Aspid.MVVM.

layers.name: Vendored: Unity Asset Store Tools
layers.description: Third-party Unity Asset Store publishing tools under Packages/com.unity.asset-store-tools used for store submission, not framework code.

layers.name: Vendored: TextMesh Pro Assets
layers.description: Bundled TextMesh Pro example fonts, sprites, and resources under _Assets used by sample scenes; third-party content.

layers.name: Documentation

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here. The root README introduces Aspid.MVVM as a high-performance, Source-Generator-based MVVM framework for Unity, and the architecture guide explains the big picture: a clean split between View, ViewModel, and business-logic, with bindings that use zero reflection. Read these first to understand WHY the framework exists before looking at HOW it works. Everything that follows fills in the details of the pipeline these documents sketch.

tour.title: The Core Contracts
tour.description: These four interfaces are the vocabulary the whole framework speaks. IViewModel is the contract the source generator implements for every [ViewModel] class (it exposes bindable members by id). IView initializes/deinitializes a View against a ViewModel. IBinder defines the bind/unbind lifecycle that connects a UI element to a value. IRelayCommand turns a method into an invokable, CanExecute-aware command. Keep this mental model: ViewModels expose members, Views own binders, binders consume members, commands trigger logic.

tour.title: Binding Modes — The Direction of Data Flow
tour.description: Building on the contracts from Step 2, BindMode is the single enum that governs which way data flows across a binding: OneWay (ViewModel to View), TwoWay (both directions), OneTime (set once), and OneWayToSource (View to ViewModel). This is the highest fan-in concept in the core: nearly every bindable member and binder is parameterized by a BindMode. The companion reference doc walks through automatic mode inference and how to override it explicitly.

tour.title: Bindable Members — The Connection Point
tour.description: A bindable member is the object on a ViewModel that a binder actually subscribes to. IReadOnlyBindableMember exposes a value plus a Changed event (covariant, for one-way display); IBindableMember adds a setter for two-way flow. The concrete classes — OneWay/TwoWay/OneTime/OneWayToSource BindableMember — are exactly the per-mode implementations the source generator emits behind each [Bind] field. This is the runtime machinery that makes zero-reflection change notification possible.

tour.title: The Generation Attributes — How Boilerplate Disappears
tour.description: Here is the heart of the framework's promise. You never hand-write IViewModel or bindable members — you annotate a partial class with these attributes and a Roslyn source generator emits the implementation at BUILD time. [ViewModel] marks the class; [Bind] (and the mode-fixed variants [OneWayBind]/[TwoWayBind]) mark fields to expose; [View] marks a View partial class for binder wiring. Compare these tiny attribute definitions with the rich members from Steps 2-4 that they cause to be generated — that gap is the boilerplate the generator writes for you.
tour.languageLesson: C# attributes are pure compile-time metadata — by themselves they do nothing at runtime. Roslyn Incremental Source Generators scan for these markers and emit additional `partial` class members during compilation. This is why every annotated class MUST be declared `partial`: the generator writes the second half. Result: zero reflection at runtime, because the generated code is plain compiled C#.

tour.title: Commands — Methods as First-Class Actions
tour.description: The [RelayCommand] attribute turns an ordinary method into a generated IRelayCommand property, optionally wired to a CanExecute predicate. RelayCommand.cs holds the concrete implementations for 0-4 parameters that the generator instantiates, wrapping your execute/canExecute delegates with shared empty singletons to avoid allocations. This is how a button-press in the View ends up invoking business logic in the ViewModel — without the View ever knowing the method name.

tour.title: The Binder Hierarchy — From Abstract Lifecycle to MonoBehaviour
tour.description: Now follow the IBinder contract from Step 2 into its implementations. Binder is the abstract base implementing the bind/unbind lifecycle with state guards and overridable hooks (OnBinding, OnBound, OnUnbinding). MonoBinder lifts that lifecycle into a Unity MonoBehaviour so binders can be dropped onto GameObjects in the Inspector, and ComponentMonoBinder specializes it to resolve and cache a typed Unity Component target. This three-level hierarchy is what lets the same binding model serve both plain C# objects and the Unity scene graph.
tour.languageLesson: This is the Template Method pattern: the base `Binder` owns the bind/unbind algorithm and state guards, exposing `protected virtual` hooks (OnBinding/OnBound/OnUnbinding) that subclasses override to plug in UI-specific behavior — without ever re-implementing the lifecycle bookkeeping.

tour.title: The View Layer — Wiring Binders to a ViewModel
tour.description: MonoView is the abstract MonoBehaviour View base, marked with [View] for source generation. It holds serialized groups of binders and, when given an IViewModel, binds each one to the matching bindable member — then unbinds on teardown. This closes the loop from Step 5's [View] attribute: the generator emits the glue, MonoView provides the runtime host. A concrete screen simply subclasses MonoView, drops binders in the Inspector, and the framework connects them.

tour.title: StarterKit — Ready-Made Binders and Converters
tour.description: The core framework is binding-agnostic; StarterKit provides the batteries. TextBinder binds string and numeric ViewModel values straight to a TMP_Text label, formatting numbers via a configurable culture. When a member's type doesn't match the UI element's type, an IConverter bridges the gap with a single Convert method — the contract every StarterKit value converter implements. The binders reference doc catalogs the full hierarchy (Text, Image, Toggle, Slider, Transform, and more) you get out of the box.

tour.title: DynamicViewModel — Binding Without Codegen
tour.description: Not every ViewModel is known at compile time. DynamicViewModel is a runtime-constructed IViewModel backed by a dictionary of IDynamicProperty instances keyed by id, resolving bindable members on demand at bind time. It is the escape hatch for data-driven UIs (think runtime-defined forms or list cells) where the [ViewModel] generator approach from Step 5 cannot apply — yet it still satisfies the exact same IViewModel contract, so every binder from Steps 7-9 works against it unchanged.

tour.title: HelloWorld — The Whole Pipeline End to End
tour.description: Everything now comes together in the smallest complete example. Speaker is a plain domain model (a Text property plus a TextChanged event). SpeakerViewModel is a [ViewModel] that wraps it and exposes a deferred Say [RelayCommand] — buffering input and committing to the model only when invoked. InputView and OutView are MonoViews whose binders connect a text input and a command to InputText/Say, and the OutText member to a label. Trace the data: keystroke to bindable member to command to model to Changed event to the output binder. This single sample exercises Steps 2 through 9.

tour.title: Build & Release — Where the Generators Live
tour.description: A capstone on how the framework is shipped. Because the Roslyn generators and analyzer are separate solutions, their compiled DLLs are committed into the Unity package and consumed at build time. The release pipeline guards this: it rebuilds the three generator/analyzer DLLs, verifies the committed copies match, validates the SemVer in package.json against the changelog, then publishes the UPM subtree. The package manifest is the contract Unity consumers install (tech.aspid.mvvm, MIT, minimum Unity version). This explains the project's key gotcha — Unity runs the DLL, not the generator source, so the DLL must always be rebuilt and committed.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows use `on` triggers (here, pushing a `v*` tag) and `jobs`/`steps` for sequential execution. The DLL-verification step is a CI guard pattern: rebuild a committed artifact from source and fail the pipeline if it differs, preventing stale generated binaries from being released.
